title: AWeber vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare AWeber and Omnisend email marketing tools. See pricing, features, ratings and which is best for your business.
h1: AWeber vs Omnisend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: AWeber and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. AWeber caters to bloggers and solopreneurs seeking simplicity, while Omnisend targets e-commerce brands wanting integrated email and SMS capabilities. This comparison will help you decide which platform best fits your marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You're a blogger or solopreneur prioritizing ease of use and simplicity
You need a larger free tier to test the platform with up to 500 subscribers
You want RSS-to-email automation without SMS complexity
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need email plus SMS in one platform
You need advanced segmentation, omnichannel automation, and pre-built workflows
You prefer higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.5+ G2, 4.6+ Trustpilot)

faq.a: Neither is universally better; it depends on your needs. AWeber excels for simplicity and content creators, offering a larger free tier and lower barrier to entry. Omnisend is superior for e-commerce brands needing integrated SMS, advanced automation, and omnichannel capabilities.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, AWeber or Omnisend?
faq.a: AWeber starts at $15/month versus Omnisend's $16/month, so AWeber has a slight edge. However, Omnisend's inclusion of SMS channels may offer better overall value for e-commerce businesses compared to purchasing SMS separately with AWeber.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to AWeber?
faq.a: Yes, most email platforms support exporting subscriber lists and contact data. Both AWeber and Omnisend allow you to export your contacts, though campaign history and automation workflows typically require manual recreation on the new platform.
